title: Droplets forming on a cold glass
description: An interactive before-and-after time-lapse where students start a cold ice-water glass and watch tiny droplets appear on its outer surface and merge into bigger drops as time advances. Replaces the hands-on observation so students can scrub time and feel the surface change.
context: Class 6 physics, observing a cold tumbler: a glass of cold ice water is left undisturbed and tiny droplets appear on its outer surface and join into bigger drops, replacing the hands-on activity so students can scrub time and feel the surface change.

theme.responsive: Fluid layout: card max-width capped by `width`, SVG scales via viewBox, panels wrap, fonts use clamp().

width.description: Maximum card width in px; layout stays fluid below this.

height.description: Advisory height; the stage is driven by the SVG viewBox.

accentColor.description: Accent (orange) colour for the touch action and wet-surface state.

animationSpeed.description: Playback speed multiplier for the time-lapse.

showTouchButton.description: Show the 'Touch the outer surface' button.

showBeatBadge.description: Show the Predict / Observe teaching-beat badge.

maxMinutes.description: Length of the time-lapse in minutes (slider range 0..maxMinutes).

dropletCount.description: Total droplets that can appear on the outer surface.

iceCubeCount.description: Floating ice cubes in the water.

startWet.description: Begin at maxMinutes with the surface already covered in droplets.

fullRunSeconds.description: Real seconds taken for a full 0->maxMinutes play (before animationSpeed).

highlightTarget.description: Visually emphasise part of the scene (used by agent highlight()).

instruction.default: Predict what happens on the dry outside of the cold glass, then play the timer and watch droplets appear and grow.

placement.where: replaces the hands-on activity of observing droplets on a cold ice-water tumbler

schema.elapsedMinutes: number — current time on the time-lapse (0..maxMinutes)
schema.dropletCount: number — how many droplets are currently visible on the surface
schema.dropletSize: number — average current droplet radius (grows as drops merge)
schema.surfaceWet: boolean — true once droplets have started forming
schema.running: boolean — whether the time-lapse is playing

window_tool_methods: setParam(name,value)  — name in: elapsedMinutes | running | surfaceWet
highlight(target)  — target in: glass | droplets | ice | surface | null
events: touch_surface  — emitted when the student touches the outer surface
state — current shared_state (emitted continuously and on getState)

student_controls.effect: Runs, halts, or restarts the time-lapse of droplets forming on the glass.
Scrubs forward so droplets appear and grow, or back so the surface returns to dry.
Reveals a cue that the outer surface feels cold and wet at that moment.

visual_spec.layout: Single tumbler centred on a soft purple-to-cream stage with a live time readout and stat cards; droplets appear on the outside and grow as time advances.

objects.name: outer droplets
objects.state: appearing, growing and merging; big drops slide down with a trail

visual_spec.palette: clear glass, pale blue water, white-blue ice, indigo/orange UI accents
scientific_facts: The outer surface starts dry and gains droplets over time.
Tiny droplets merge into bigger drops as time passes.
The outer surface feels cold and wet once droplets form.

teaching_notes: At Predict, ask what will appear on the dry outside; play at Observe to reveal growing droplets; explain condensation in the next topic.
instructions_for_student: Predict what happens on the dry outside of the cold glass, then play the timer and watch droplets appear and grow.

tool_call_examples.name: Default observe (Predict -> Observe)
tool_call_examples.instructions_for_student: Predict, then play and watch the droplets form.

tool_call_examples.name: Show the finished, fully-wet glass
tool_call_examples.instructions_for_student: This is how the glass looks after a few minutes — covered in droplets.

tool_call_examples.name: Faster time-lapse with more droplets
tool_call_examples.instructions_for_student: Watch a busier surface fill up quickly.

tool_call_examples.name: Minimal display for a focused observation
tool_call_examples.instructions_for_student: Just watch — the droplets are highlighted as they grow.
